macro_summary: U.S. stocks tumbled Tuesday, with the Nasdaq plunging 3.1% and SOXX down 7.5% as AI-linked semiconductor names led a sharp sell-off. A fresh hawkish call from BofA—projecting 75 bps of Fed rate hikes in 2026—combined with the Strait of Hormuz remaining half-open to rattle confidence. Tech giant Google bucked the trend after-hours on news it will replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average.
commodities_note: WTI crude held at $73.09 (+1.0%) amid contradictory signals from Hormuz—transit remains sporadic but a U.S.-Iran roadmap is still in play. Gold rebounded 0.8% to $4,102.40, reflecting lingering geopolitical and inflation anxiety. The US 10-year yield slipped to 4.49%.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains mechanically blocked across DBS, OCBC, and UOB with RSI readings near overbought and NIM headwinds from elevated US rates and S$NEER appreciation. OCBC’s Indonesia wealth-adviser expansion (per Private Banker International) is a micro positive but does not alter the macro rate exposure.
singapore.key_development: Singapore May CPI remained at 1.8%, offering potential NIM relief, but the US10Y needs to drop below 4.20% to materially ease funding costs.

semiconductors.summary: The semiconductor cluster took a heavy hit with SOXX plunging 7.5%, led by MU (-14.5%) and broad AI spending fears. NVDA and GOOG are the only names near technical entry zones, while Intel's foundry-gap failure reinforces the need for proved external customer wins. The cluster's high dispersion favors selective bottom-fishing over sector-basket exposure.
semiconductors.key_development: Micron’s imminent earnings (tomorrow) and the SK Hynix KOSPI rout are testing the HBM demand thesis; NVDA below its 50-day SMA offers the first actionable entry in semis in weeks.

big_tech.summary: GOOG & MSFT are the standout stories within BigTech, with GOOG upgraded to BUY on DJIA inclusion and TPU momentum, while MSFT remains a watch with Azure organic recovery signs. AMZN and PLTR still face defensive positioning, but the cluster is no longer uniformly cautious. The divergence from semis—where most names remain extended—highlights defensive rotation into mega-cap software/cloud.
big_tech.thesis_status: Selectively bullish — GOOG leads, MSFT coiling
big_tech.key_development: Google’s DJIA replacement of Verizon and the Anthropic-GOOG TPU deal provide a near-term catalyst for re-rating, isolating it from the AI-capital-expenditure hangover.

interconnected.reason: AI networking/ASIC play, levered to hyperscaler capex; down 9%+ with semis. Its custom ASIC pipeline benefits from diversification away from NVDA, linking to AVGO and GOOG.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for a bounce above its 50-day moving average after Micron earnings confirm HBM demand trajectory; then add as a semis proxy.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: HBM competitor to SK Hynix and Micron; KOSPI rout presents a contrarian opportunity if HBM cycle remains strong. Linked to 000660.KS and MU.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if Samsung drops to ₩50,000 (approx 200-day) as a low-bar entry ahead of HBM qualification news; use MU earnings as a catalyst signal.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking upgrades—Arista is a direct beneficiary alongside NOK; hyperscaler switch spend accelerates with AI cluster scaling.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AI capex commentary from hyperscalers reaccelerates post-Micron; watch $350 support as a base.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains half-open after Iran’s re-closure threat; transit sporadic, WTI volatile near $73, and the U.S.-Iran roadmap still fragile.
U.S.-China tech war escalates with China’s export curbs on American defense firms and ASML EUV suspicions, risking broader semiconductor supply disruption.
Fed hawkish pivot under Chair Warsh—BofA projects 75 bps of 2026 hikes—pressuring rate-sensitive tech valuations; May PCE data on Thursday is the next catalyst.
AI capex sustainability fears hit mega-cap tech and semis; Micron’s earnings tomorrow will test the HBM demand narrative.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil volatility from Hormuz half-open status keeps energy prices unstable, pressuring tech multiples. Fed hike fears and hawkish Warsh FOMC have lifted real yields, hitting semis hardest. China’s retaliatory export controls add supply-chain risk for ASML and defense-linked tech names.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: BofA forecast of three 2026 rate hikes; Google to replace Verizon in DJIA; Micron earnings tomorrow; China’s export curbs on US defense firms; KOSPI rout on leveraged ETF selling (per Bloomberg).

base.description: Hormuz stalemate persists with sporadic oil shipments; Fed holds rates but hawkish rhetoric keeps US10Y near 4.5%, capping tech valuations. Semis remain volatile, with advantage to those near support like NVDA.

optimistic.description: US-Iran final deal leads to full Hormuz reopening, oil slides toward $65, easing inflation fears and allowing Fed to pause. Semis rally as risk sentiment improves; GOOG, NVDA lead.

pessimistic.description: Iran closes strait again, oil spikes to $90+, inflation surges, Fed forced to hike, sending semis into another leg down. Defensive rotation into mega-cap cash-rich names like GOOG.

wildcard.description: Unexpected China chip ban escalates into a broader tech war, cutting off ASML and HBM supply, causing a VIX spike above 30 and a flight to gold.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain cash with selective entry on oversold tech (GOOG, NVDA) at support; avoid extended semis and banks. No new positions ahead of Micron earnings.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 10 of 28 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, but the tech sell-off has created oversold setups in NVDA and GOOG. Two stepped-down (AXTI, GOOG signal changes) and one earnings beat (CBRS) fail to shift the macro overhang from Hormuz and rate fears.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=0) / ACCUMULATE (n=5) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
